(born November 27, 1971)[[3]](#cite_note-3) is an American actor best known as [Miguel Alvarez](/wiki/Miguel_Alvarez_(Oz) "Miguel Alvarez (Oz)") in *[Oz](/wiki/Oz_(TV_series) "Oz (TV series)")*, [Joe Toye](/wiki/Joe_Toye "Joe Toye") in *[Band of Brothers](/wiki/Band_of_Brothers_(miniseries) "Band of Brothers (miniseries)")*, and [FBI](/wiki/FBI "FBI") Agent [Charlie Francis](/wiki/Charlie_Francis_(Fringe) "Charlie Francis (Fringe)") on *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")*. His best-known films are *[The Thin Red Line](/wiki/The_Thin_Red_Line_(1998_film) "The Thin Red Line (1998 film)")*, *[Dinner Rush](/wiki/Dinner_Rush "Dinner Rush")* and *[Dawn of the Planet of the Apes](/wiki/Dawn_of_the_Planet_of_the_Apes "Dawn of the Planet of the Apes")*. He also was José Ramse on *[12 Monkeys](/wiki/12_Monkeys_(TV_series) "12 Monkeys (TV series)")* (2015–18), as well as [Ricardo Diaz / The Dragon](/wiki/Ricardo_Diaz_(Arrowverse) "Ricardo Diaz (Arrowverse)") on *[Arrow](/wiki/Arrow_(TV_series) "Arrow (TV series)")* (2017–19).

American actor (born 1971)

**Kirkland M. Acevedo** (born November 27, 1971)[[3]](#cite_note-3) is an American actor best known as [Miguel Alvarez](/wiki/Miguel_Alvarez_(Oz) "Miguel Alvarez (Oz)") in *[Oz](/wiki/Oz_(TV_series) "Oz (TV series)")*, [Joe Toye](/wiki/Joe_Toye "Joe Toye") in *[Band of Brothers](/wiki/Band_of_Brothers_(miniseries) "Band of Brothers (miniseries)")*, and [FBI](/wiki/FBI "FBI") Agent [Charlie Francis](/wiki/Charlie_Francis_(Fringe) "Charlie Francis (Fringe)") on *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")*. His best-known films are *[The Thin Red Line](/wiki/The_Thin_Red_Line_(1998_film) "The Thin Red Line (1998 film)")*, *[Dinner Rush](/wiki/Dinner_Rush "Dinner Rush")* and *[Dawn of the Planet of the Apes](/wiki/Dawn_of_the_Planet_of_the_Apes "Dawn of the Planet of the Apes")*. He also was José Ramse on *[12 Monkeys](/wiki/12_Monkeys_(TV_series) "12 Monkeys (TV series)")* (2015–18), as well as [Ricardo Diaz / The Dragon](/wiki/Ricardo_Diaz_(Arrowverse) "Ricardo Diaz (Arrowverse)") on *[Arrow](/wiki/Arrow_(TV_series) "Arrow (TV series)")* (2017–19).

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kirk_Acevedo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Acevedo's parents were born in New York City and lived in [the Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx "The Bronx"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) where both their sons, Richard and Kirk Acevedo, were born and raised. Acevedo is of [Puerto Rican](/wiki/Puerto_Ricans "Puerto Ricans") descent.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Since childhood, Acevedo has shown an interest in acting; he performed before his family in improvised shows. Acevedo was a drama major in high school.

After graduating from [LaGuardia High School of Music & Art and Performing Arts](/wiki/Fiorello_H._LaGuardia_High_School_of_Music_%26_Art_and_Performing_Arts "Fiorello H. LaGuardia High School of Music & Art and Performing Arts"), Acevedo enrolled in the [SUNY Purchase School of Acting](/wiki/Purchase_College "Purchase College") (Purchase College). In the 1990s, Acevedo earned his [Bachelor of Fine Arts](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Fine_Arts "Bachelor of Fine Arts") degree and, as some of his classmates had landed parts shortly after auditioning for *[Oz](/wiki/Oz_(TV_series) "Oz (TV series)")*, he was also motivated to try out, and subsequently landed the role of the half-crazed gang leader prisoner [Miguel Alvarez](/wiki/Miguel_Alvarez_(Oz) "Miguel Alvarez (Oz)").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kirk_Acevedo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

After landing the role of Miguel Alvarez on *[Oz](/wiki/Oz_(TV_series) "Oz (TV series)")*, Acevedo was promoted to series regular in season two and appeared in the following episodes: 1–26; 34–36 and 41–56.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Among his other most notable credits include his roles in *[Law & Order: Trial by Jury](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Trial_by_Jury "Law & Order: Trial by Jury")* (2005) as [Hector Salazar](/wiki/Hector_Salazar_(Law_%26_Order) "Hector Salazar (Law & Order)") and [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO")'s *[Band of Brothers](/wiki/Band_of_Brothers_(miniseries) "Band of Brothers (miniseries)")* (2001), produced by [Steven Spielberg](/wiki/Steven_Spielberg "Steven Spielberg"), as Staff sergeant [Joe Toye](/wiki/Joe_Toye "Joe Toye").

He had guest starring roles on well known television shows such as *[NYPD Blue](/wiki/NYPD_Blue "NYPD Blue")* and *[24](/wiki/24_(TV_series) "24 (TV series)")*. He had a notable recurring role on the television show *[The Black Donnellys](/wiki/The_Black_Donnellys "The Black Donnellys")*.

Some of his most well known movies and [miniseries](/wiki/Miniseries "Miniseries") he has starred in include *[The Thin Red Line](/wiki/The_Thin_Red_Line_(1998_film) "The Thin Red Line (1998 film)")*, *[Invincible](/wiki/Invincible_(2006_film) "Invincible (2006 film)")* (2006), *[Boiler Room](/wiki/Boiler_Room_(film) "Boiler Room (film)")* (2000), *[Dinner Rush](/wiki/Dinner_Rush "Dinner Rush")* (2000), *Arresting Gena* (1997) and *Kirk and Kerry* (1997). He voices Jackie Estacado in *[The Darkness](/wiki/The_Darkness_(video_game) "The Darkness (video game)")*, the video game adaptation of the [comic book](/wiki/The_Darkness_(character) "The Darkness (character)").

In 2008, he landed a supporting role on the [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") science fiction horror television series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* as [Charlie Francis](/wiki/Charlie_Francis_(Fringe) "Charlie Francis (Fringe)"). He starred on the show's [first season](/wiki/Fringe_(season_1) "Fringe (season 1)") and in the episode "[Unleashed](/wiki/Unleashed_(Fringe) "Unleashed (Fringe)")", Acevedo's real-life wife, actress [Kiersten Warren](/wiki/Kiersten_Warren "Kiersten Warren") portrayed his on-screen wife. His character was killed in the [second season premiere](/wiki/A_New_Day_in_the_Old_Town "A New Day in the Old Town"), though Acevedo made two further appearances portraying a shape-shifting imposter. Following the [fifth episode](/wiki/Dream_Logic "Dream Logic") of [season two](/wiki/Fringe_(season_2) "Fringe (season 2)"), Acevedo was no longer credited as a main cast member due to the shapeshifter's death in the [previous episode](/wiki/Momentum_Deferred "Momentum Deferred"). Though he is credited as a main cast member for a standalone episode filmed at the end of the first season titled "[Unearthed](/wiki/Unearthed_(Fringe) "Unearthed (Fringe)")" that was broadcast as the eleventh episode of the second season. His character's reappearance in the episode after being killed off has led to confusion among viewers of the series.

Acevedo officially returned as a guest star in *Fringe* for the [second season finale](/wiki/Over_There_(Fringe) "Over There (Fringe)") as the parallel universe version of his character, and continued this recurring role for six episodes of the [third season](/wiki/Fringe_(season_3) "Fringe (season 3)").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In 2011, he joined the main cast of the short-lived American remake of *[Prime Suspect](/wiki/Prime_Suspect_(American_TV_series) "Prime Suspect (American TV series)")* portraying Detective Luisito Calderon.[[8]](#cite_note-Prime2011-8) Initially, it was reported Alt-Charlie would appear in fewer episodes of the [fourth season](/wiki/Fringe_(season_4) "Fringe (season 4)") of *Fringe*, though this never happened and he never reappeared in the series following the third season episode "[Bloodline](/wiki/Bloodline_(Fringe) "Bloodline (Fringe)")".[[8]](#cite_note-Prime2011-8) Acevedo later appeared on *[The Walking Dead](/wiki/The_Walking_Dead_(TV_series) "The Walking Dead (TV series)")* for two episodes of the [fourth season](/wiki/The_Walking_Dead_(season_4) "The Walking Dead (season 4)").

He has starred in the [Syfy](/wiki/Syfy "Syfy") television series *[12 Monkeys](/wiki/12_Monkeys_(TV_series) "12 Monkeys (TV series)")*. Acevedo was cast in a recurring role in the sixth season of [The CW](/wiki/The_CW "The CW") television series *[Arrow](/wiki/Arrow_(TV_series) "Arrow (TV series)")* in August 2017 as [Ricardo Diaz](/wiki/Ricardo_Diaz_(Arrowverse) "Ricardo Diaz (Arrowverse)")[[9]](#cite_note-9) and was promoted to series regular for the first 14 episodes of [season seven](/wiki/Arrow_season_7 "Arrow season 7").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Accolades

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kirk_Acevedo&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Accolades")]

In 1999, Acevedo won an [ALMA Award](/wiki/ALMA_Award "ALMA Award") for his role in *[The Thin Red Line](/wiki/The_Thin_Red_Line_(1998_film) "The Thin Red Line (1998 film)")*. He was nominated in the "Outstanding Featured Actor in a Play" category in 1997 for the Drama Desk Award for *Tooth of Crime*. He was nominated for a total of four [ALMA Awards](/wiki/ALMA_Award "ALMA Award") for his role in *Oz* for the following years 1997, 1998, 2000, and 2001.[[11]](#cite_note-11) He co-founded the theater company, The Rorschach Group, with [Shea Whigham](/wiki/Shea_Whigham "Shea Whigham").
